name: Encoding Utilities
creator: Various standards bodies
modern_use: Data transport, binary-to-text conversion, URL parameters, email attachments (MIME)
description: Encoding transforms data into a different representation for transport or storage compatibility. It is NOT encryption - anyone can decode it. Base64 encodes binary data as ASCII text. Hex represents bytes as hexadecimal digits. URL encoding escapes special characters in URLs.
notes: Base64 increases data size by ~33%. Never use encoding as a security measure.
did_you_know: Base64 was invented for MIME email attachments in 1987. The alphabet uses A-Z, a-z, 0-9, + and / with = as padding.
